free.name: Evaluation
free.description: Talk to demo employees. Hire your own on a paid plan.
free.features: 2 demo employees: Adeline + Yuki|30 Talk minutes / month|AI chat|No custom employees

starter.name: Starter
starter.description: Your first custom digital employees. Not a toy chat seat.
starter.features: Starter catalog: Adeline + Yuki|3 custom digital employees|Up to 120 Talk minutes / month|Up to 5,000 knowledge chunks|1 custom avatar included|Dialog history

studio.name: Studio
studio.description: A personal digital team you actually run day to day.
studio.features: Starter catalog: Adeline + Yuki|8 custom digital employees|Up to 400 Talk minutes / month|Up to 25,000 knowledge chunks|2 custom avatars included|History and dialog search

operator.name: Team
operator.description: A department of digital employees with seats and read API.
operator.features: Full NULLXES employee marketplace access|20 custom digital employees|Up to 1,500 Talk minutes / month|Shared workspace · 10 seats|Up to 100,000 knowledge chunks|Read API

scale.name: Scale
scale.description: Grow a real digital workforce with full API access.
scale.features: Full NULLXES catalog|50 custom digital employees|Up to 5,000 Talk minutes / month|Full API|Up to 20 custom avatars|Priority support

discovery.name: Discovery
discovery.description: Processes, architecture, and a digital employee deployment plan.
discovery.features: Business process analysis|Automation map|1–2 working prototypes|NULLXES deployment architect

pilot.name: Pilot
pilot.description: First digital employees in the company’s operating environment.
pilot.features: 3–10 digital employees|Custom avatars and voices|Corporate systems integration|Metrics and reporting

department.name: Digital department
department.description: A business unit run by digital employees under unified control.
department.features: Unit automation|Role and access control|Analytics and activity log|SSO and priority support

holding.name: Digital holding
holding.description: Manage digital employees across multiple organizations.
holding.features: Multiple organizations|Hundreds of digital employees|Data security requirements|Dedicated engineering team and SLA

flagship.name: Digital corporation
flagship.features: Private digital corporation|Private infrastructure|Custom AI models and services|Dedicated NULLXES team

workforce.label: Workforce

workforce.subtitle: Specialized digital employees. Roles, not chat windows.

enterprise.label: Enterprise
enterprise.title: Built for institutions that cannot afford a wrong answer.

controlledDeployment.title: Controlled deployment
controlledDeployment.body: Digital employees launch inside your org boundaries — roles, channels, and approval paths stay explicit.

operationalContinuity.title: Operational continuity
operationalContinuity.body: First contact, routine questions, and handoffs keep moving when the human floor is offline.

accountablePresence.title: Accountable presence
accountablePresence.body: Every interaction is attributable: who spoke, what knowledge was used, what requires a human.

identityAccess.title: Identity & access
identityAccess.body: Role-based permissions, secure authentication, and controlled workspace access for every team.

infrastructureControl.title: Infrastructure control
infrastructureControl.body: Manage API keys, IP allowlists, webhooks, and external integrations from a single control layer.

sessionGovernance.title: Session governance
sessionGovernance.body: Control session limits, usage budgets, and maintain a complete audit trail for every digital employee.

pricing.label: Pricing
pricing.title: Hire capacity. Not seats for chat.

organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.intro: Data and limits are scoped to an organization.
isolationItems: UI and API requests are scoped by organizationId
NULLXES catalog — read for Talk, definition immutable
organizations.modelTree:
Organization
├ Owner (membership)
├ Members (admin / operator / viewer)
├ Digital employees
├ Knowledge / Missions / Sessions
├ API keys
└ Billing plan + limits

workspaces.title: Workspaces
workspaces.intro: Workspace is the user session context: active organization, permissions, billing.
workspaces.overviewTree:
User
  ↓ membership
Organization
  ↓ workspace context
Dashboard / Talk / API
workspaces.overviewDetail: One user can belong to multiple organizations. The active workspace is selected at sign-in. See also:

accessControl.title: Access control
accessControl.rbacText: Permissions are derived from membership role. Matrix:
isolationItems: All business entities are bound to organizationId
Every Public API key belongs to one org

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.intro: Secondary layer relative to the workforce. The dashboard shows employee activity, sessions, and limit usage.
overviewItems: Does not replace audit log — see Audit

audit.title: Audit log
audit.intro: Records significant actions in the organization: keys, members, export, settings.

integrations.title: Integrations
integrations.intro: Platform extension points for external systems.
integrations.avatarItem: Avatar / voice providers — provisioning via the platform (not client lab keys)
integrations.billingItem: Billing webhooks (Polar / T-Bank) — internal platform hooks

missions.title: Missions
missions.intro: Scheduled and manual tasks for a digital employee outside live Talk.
overviewItems: Skill IDs / schedule — per mission configuration
missions.runtimeText: Runs via platform background workers. Results and statuses are available in the missions UI. For external automation —

knowledge.title: Knowledge
knowledge.intro: Digital employee knowledge base: sources are indexed and used in Talk (RAG).
knowledge.overviewText: Knowledge sources are bound to an employee. Indexing runs via Inngest background jobs. During a session, retrieval injects relevant fragments into the brain-stream context.
knowledge.limitsText: Maximum knowledge chunks are set by plan. Matrix:

apiSecurity.title: API Security
keysItems: Bearer / header auth with nx_live_ key

changelog.title: Documentation changelog
changelog.intro: Changes to the /docs portal and Public API. Portal version: 2.1.0.
v210Items: IA developer portal: Start · Workforce · Platform · Admin · Security · Legal · Developers · Enterprise
New pages: architecture, organizations, roles, API keys, webhooks, limits, security, enterprise
Assistant: Yuki Nakora (no model branding in UI)
v200Items: First /docs portal + Public API human docs

privacy.title: Privacy policy
privacy.intro: Short index. Full personal data package — in personal data and terms sections.

apiKeys.title: API Keys
apiKeys.introPrefix: Organization keys for the Public API. Prefix
apiKeys.introSuffix: . The full value is shown once on creation.
createItems: Access depends on plan (none / read / full)
apiKeys.scopesBundles: Bundles: Read-only · Workforce Operator · Admin Integration. See
apiKeys.revokeBody: Revoke — immediate invalidation. Rotation: create a new key → switch integrations → revoke the old one. Store secrets outside the repository.

cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.intro: NULLXES Digital Employees uses cookies and similar technologies to run the platform, save preferences, and (with consent) anonymized visit analytics.
cookies.categoryNecessaryBody: — Better Auth session, UI language (NEXT_LOCALE), consent record (nx_cookie_consent). Without them, sign-in and basic site operation are impossible or severely limited.
cookies.categoryAnalyticsBody: — Vercel Analytics only after choosing Accept in the consent banner. No ad tracking or third-party profiling cookies.
cookies.consentBody1: On first visit a banner is shown. The choice is stored in the first-party cookie
cookies.consentBody3: To change your decision, delete the cookie in the browser and refresh the page — the banner will appear again.

dataStorage.title: Data storage
dataStorage.intro: For Enterprise and 152-FZ path — where and how data lives.
whereItems: Object storage — avatars / knowledge files (per configuration)
dataRegion on org — product flag; RU contour — separate deployment
dataStorage.whereNote: We do not claim residency in Russia until a separate RU DB contour is connected.
dataStorage.retentionExport: Organization data export — Settings / security flows
dataStorage.retentionPurge: Deletion / purge — on request and per retention policies

employees.title: Digital employees
employees.intro: The core platform entity. Everything else is services around the workforce.
modelItems: Talk — runtime session with a user
employees.catalogBody: Published platform employees (for example Adeline, Yuki) are available for Talk. Their definition is immutable: changing the definition via Public API and UI is prohibited. Custom employees are created within plan limits.

enterprise.title: Enterprise
enterprise.intro: Contour for banks, holdings, and regulated deployments. Some items are available now, some are roadmap (clearly marked).
deploymentItems: RU data contour — separate DB + object storage (cutover)

roles.title: Users and roles
roles.intro: Membership role in an organization. Implemented: owner, admin, operator, viewer.
roles.roleOwner: Owner — organization, billing, API keys, webhooks, org deletion

security.title: Platform security
security.intro: Controls without fluff. What actually exists in the product.

talk.title: Talk
talk.intro: Premium dialogue with a digital employee: text and voice. Cognition belongs to the platform; the Anam avatar is the visual layer.
startSteps: Make sure the status is Talk Ready
Click Talk and start a session
End the session with the End session button
talk.limitsBody: Session duration and monthly minute budget depend on the plan. See
talk.readyBody: Readiness = avatar provisioning ready + session provisioning ready. If Talk is unavailable — see

troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
troubleshooting.introPrefix: Common failures and what to check. If that does not help —
troubleshooting.talkNotReady: No Talk Ready — wait for avatar/session provisioning
troubleshooting.talkLimitsPrefix: Minute limit — check your plan at
apiItems: 403 — insufficient scopes or plan without API (Evaluation/Starter/Studio)
missionsItems: Status not updating — refresh the page (Inngest async)
Evaluation: create/missions for catalog are limited
billingItems: No Create button on Evaluation — expected (catalog only)
Checkout — Starter / Studio / Team / Scale; Enterprise — Contact sales

webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.intro: Outbound organization events to your HTTPS endpoint. HMAC-SHA256 signature.

overview.title: Overview
overview.intro: Documentation for NULLXES Digital Employees — for clients, integrators, security teams, and developers. One topic per page. No tutorials on how to use a computer.

sections.title: Architecture
sections.description: User → Workspace → Employees → Knowledge → Talk → LLM → Logs.

sections.title: Security
sections.description: TLS, RBAC, audit, keys, signed webhooks.

api.title: Public API v1
api.intro: REST API for external integrations: employees, Talk sessions, and task assignment. Contract — OpenAPI; keys are created in Settings → Security.
api.overviewNote: The public surface is /api/v1/* only. Internal Talk, Anam, Inngest, billing, and auth routes are not part of Public API.
authSteps: Create an API key (organization owner permissions required)
Choose a bundle: Read-only, Workforce Operator, or Admin Integration
Pass the key only in the Authorization header
api.planNote: API access depends on plan: Evaluation / Starter / Studio — no keys; Team — read; Scale / Enterprise / Holding — full access.
api.sdkIntro: When source access is granted (on request), the client is generated from public/openapi.yaml:
api.sdkImport: Import: @/features/public-api/sdk. OpenAPI spec is also public: GET /api/docs.
securityItems: Do not pass the key in query string or client bundle unless necessary
Limit scopes to the minimally required bundle
Enable IP allowlist in Settings → Security when needed
Access denials are recorded in Audit (api.access.denied)

architecture.title: Platform architecture
architecture.intro: NULLXES Digital Employees — a digital workforce operating system. Diagrams in Mermaid (source of truth for agents and review).
stackItems: App — Next.js 16 App Router, React 19, TypeScript
architecture.c4Text: The operator uses the web app; API and workers split synchronous and background paths over one database.
architecture.talkText: Each turn receives a live snapshot of missions/tasks; read-tools are always available to the model.
architecture.erdText: Catalog employees are published via platform_employee_catalog and are not duplicated in the home-org list.

functional.title: Functional characteristics
functional.intro: NULLXES Digital Employees is an enterprise platform for creating, managing, and operating digital employees in organizational business processes.
functional.purposeText: NULLXES enables organizations to deploy digital employees for customer inquiries, internal knowledge, tasks, communications, and operational scenarios. The platform combines role configuration, action control, analytics, and integrations in a single operational environment.
capabilitiesItems: Digital employee management — profile creation, role definition, responsibility scope, tone of voice, and interaction scenarios.
Dialogues and inquiry handling — text and voice sessions, interaction history, and communication quality control.
Corporate knowledge — connecting organizational documents and materials for use within permitted work context.
Tasks and workflows — assigning tasks to digital employees, tracking status, routing results, and handoff to responsible specialists.
Control and approval — review of actions requiring human involvement, plus management of outbound communications and execution rules.
Operations center — unified space for monitoring digital workforce activity, current tasks, and key events.
Analytics — usage, activity, dialogue, task, and knowledge base metrics for evaluating deployment effectiveness.
Integrations — connection to corporate systems and external services via API according to organizational policies.
functional.governanceText1: The platform provides tools for managing organizations, workspaces, members, and access rights. Digital employee settings, data processing rules, and interaction scenarios are controlled at the organization level.
functional.governanceText2: For critical operations, human-in-the-loop may apply: result approval, action confirmation, or handoff to a responsible specialist.
securityItems: User access separation within the organization.
Management of access keys and integration connections.
Monitoring of events and activity in the workspace.
Configuration of access policies and network interaction restrictions.
Data export and retention management within available configuration.
functional.deploymentText1: NULLXES supports phased deployment: from evaluating a single scenario to scaling digital employees across customer service, sales, HR, support, and internal operations.
functional.deploymentText2: Integration scope, security requirements, access procedures, and support parameters are defined within the selected enterprise contour. For text generation and analysis, organizations may connect various LLM providers (including OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and the platform NULLXES brain) via Settings → AI.
functional.roleAdmin: Administrator — manages the organization, members, access policies, settings, and integrations.
functional.roleUser: User — works with digital employees, dialogues, tasks, and available work materials within granted permissions.

installation.title: Quick start
installation.intro: The software is deployed as a web application (SaaS). No end-user installation is required — a modern browser is sufficient. Below are requirements and deployment steps for infrastructure administrators.
requirementsItems: Hosting with Next.js support (Vercel or equivalent)
Inngest Cloud access for background jobs
OpenAI API key for LLM features
installation.sourcePolicy: Platform source code is not publicly available. Access is granted only upon request to the rights holder.
installation.envNote: The full list of variables is provided together with source access (on request).
verifySteps: Sign in at /login, create an organization

limits.title: Platform limits
knowledgeItems: maxKnowledgeChunks — index per organization (see plan matrix)

offer.label: Public offer
offer.title: Offer agreement for access to the {brand} platform
offer.intro: This document is an official offer to conclude an agreement on the terms below. Payment for a plan or registration with acceptance of the terms constitutes acceptance of the offer (Civil Code of the Russian Federation, Articles 435, 438).
offer.subjectText: The Provider grants the Customer access to the {brand} Digital Employees cloud platform (creation and operation of digital employees) under the selected plan. The service is provided remotely via
offer.pricesItem1Prefix: Current self-serve plan prices (Evaluation / Studio / Team / Scale) are published in
offer.pricesItem1Suffix: and in platform Billing settings (in rubles).
offer.pricesItem2: Enterprise plans (Discovery, Pilot, Department, Holding, Flagship) — by individual agreement.
offer.pricesItem3: Access for the paid period (month or year) opens after payment confirmation. The digital service is provided immediately after plan activation.
orderSteps: Register an account or sign in to your workspace.
In Settings → Billing, select a plan and period (month / year).
Pay via the bank payment form (card, SBP, and other methods available on the terminal).
After successful payment, the plan is activated; an electronic receipt is issued when an online cash register is connected (Federal Law 54-FZ).
offer.noPhysicalDelivery: Physical goods are not delivered — the service is digital.
offer.refundItem1Suffix: with your account email, OrderId / PaymentId, and reason.
refundItems: Claims are reviewed within 10 business days. Full or partial refunds are returned via the bank using the same payment method.
If plan access has been substantially used (employee creation, Talk, API), refunds may be proportional or declined — with a reasoned response.
Parties seek to resolve disputes through negotiation; if no agreement is reached — at the Provider's location.

operation.title: Operations guide
operation.intro: User and administrator guide for operating NULLXES Digital Employees in the production environment nullxesdai.online.
loginSteps: Enter email and password or register at /register
If 2FA is enabled, confirm the code (Settings → Security)
createSteps: Sidebar → Digital Employees → New Digital Employee
Specify name, role, department, system prompt
In Studio, configure Brain provider (OpenAI GPT recommended for production), model, avatar, voice
talkSteps: Start a text or voice session
Responses are formed using system prompt and knowledge base (RAG)
missionsSteps: Select an employee (for example, Yuki Nakora)
Brief — text assignment, for example: «Find 10 B2B companies in Europe that could benefit from NULLXES Digital Employees…»
On the mission page, track Timeline: research → leads → approval → outbound → handoff
Settings → Security → Approvals — approve proposals before outbound send
operation.missionsNote: Mission processing runs in the background via Inngest. Refresh the mission page manually (F5) for the current status.
operation.settingsAi: Settings → AI — default brain provider, model, own API keys (OpenAI)
operation.settingsSecurity: Settings → Security — 2FA, API keys, IP allowlist, approvals
operation.apiIntro: REST API v1 — employees, Talk sessions, and tasks. Keys: Settings → Security. Full description of scopes, endpoints, and examples — in
apiItems: Plan: Evaluation/Starter/Studio — no API; Team — read; Scale+ — full access
monitoringItems: Inngest Dashboard — Runs, Events, cron jobs

personalData.title: Personal data (152-FZ)
personalData.intro1: Documentation on personal data processing and protection is prepared in accordance with Federal Law No. 152-FZ of 27.07.2006 «On Personal Data», GOST R ISO/IEC 27001-2021 (information security management systems), and GOST R 7.0.8-2013 (records management / documentation).
personalData.intro2: This section describes the personal data operator, categories processed, storage locations and conditions, protection measures, access audit, and data subject rights. Legally significant consent forms and local operator acts apply in addition to this public description.
categoriesItems: Workspace user account data: name, email, role, authentication details (including 2FA when enabled)
Organizational data: organization profile, team members, invitations
Digital workforce operational data: digital employee profiles, Talk/Conversations sessions, tasks, missions, knowledge sources
Technical logs: audit events, IP (when recorded), API access metadata
Payment/plan data to the extent required for billing (via payment provider)
personalData.categoriesNote: Special categories of personal data and biometric data are not requested by default. Avatar portrait uploads are organization content and are processed within digital employee operation purposes.
documentsItems: Order appointing the person responsible for personal data processing and protection
Order approving the list of storage locations for personal data documents and media
Personal data processing and protection policy (published on the website)
Personal data disclosure to third parties log
personalData.storageElectronicText: User personal and operational data is stored in PostgreSQL (Neon) with organization-scoped access. Deployment region is determined by configuration; enterprise options account for localization requirements.
personalData.storageAccessText: Access only through individual Better Auth accounts, workspace RBAC roles (owner / admin / operator / viewer), optional 2FA for administrators, and API keys with scoped access.
personalData.storageEncryptionText: Sensitive fields (webhook secrets, integration tokens, export tokens, organization API keys) are encrypted at rest (AES-256-GCM). Details —
personalData.storageRetentionText: Processing and storage cease when processing purposes are achieved, consent expires, or consent is withdrawn. Organization retention policy governs session retention; destruction is commission-based with a personal data destruction act. Workspace owners may initiate organization data export and deletion in Settings → Advanced.
personalData.auditIntro: In accordance with requirements for logging actions during personal data processing, the platform maintains an audit log of security-relevant organization events: security setting changes, API key operations, data export, API access denial, administrative actions.
auditItems: View and filter: Settings → Security / Audit (for authorized roles with organization management rights)
Record contents: action, actor (user/role), resource type, resource identifier, metadata, IP (if recorded), timestamp
The log is for internal operator control and incident investigation; it is not public live monitoring of Trust Center
Digital workforce work events (tasks, approvals, handoff) are also recorded in the organization operational contour
personalData.auditIncident: In an information security incident, the operator follows the response plan from the 152-FZ document set and notifies data subjects / the authorized body when required by law.
personalData.rightsTextPrefix: A data subject may request access, clarification, blocking, or destruction of their data, and withdraw consent (Articles 14, 21 of 152-FZ and related norms). Requests are accepted at
personalData.rightsTextSuffix: Review period — within limits established by Russian Federation law.
personalData.referencePrefix: Reference materials on the 152-FZ document set:

plans.title: Plans and limits
plans.introSuffix: Self-serve: Evaluation → Starter → Studio → Team → Scale. Sales: Enterprise, Holding.
plans.evaluationNote: * Evaluation: creating your own employees is disabled — curated NULLXES catalog (Talk allowed). Catalog is not counted in maxEmployees.
apiAccessItems: Evaluation / Starter / Studio — keys are not created

terms.title: Terms of service
terms.intro: This agreement governs access to the NULLXES Digital Employees platform and use of its functionality. By registering an account, you confirm that you have read and fully accept these terms.
scopeItems: The platform provides tools for creating, configuring, and operating digital employees in organization workspaces.
Access is granted to registered users within the scope of their role and workspace plan limits.
terms.scopePersonalDataPrefix: Personal data processing is governed separately — see the
obligationsItems: Do not share account credentials with third parties and maintain password confidentiality.
Do not use the service for unlawful activities, spam, malicious code, or bypassing technical restrictions.
Respect third-party rights when uploading materials to the knowledge base and dialogue scenarios.
terms.liabilityText: The service is provided «as is». The operator is not liable for decisions made by the user based on AI agent responses, or for interruptions of third-party providers (LLM, avatars, email), subject to reasonable efforts to restore operability.
